message:
fix(abi): translate managed condattr to host format before cond_init delegation

Same class of bug as the mutexattr fix (537bad6): our pthread_condattr_t
custom encoding (valid bit at bit 2, clock at bit 0, pshared at bit 1)
differs from glibc's internal layout. When delegating pthread_cond_init
to host with our encoded condattr, glibc would misinterpret the fields.

Fix: when the condattr has our valid bit set, create a fresh glibc-format
condattr via pthread_condattr_init + pthread_condattr_setclock, then pass
that to the host.

Note: 2 pre-existing pthread_cond_core_test failures remain (condvar_init
_accepts_initialized_attr, condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock) —
these were failing before this change and are a separate issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
